moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% to boost income and broad market exposure.
Increase QQQ to 25% for additional broad index exposure.
Trim SMH to 5% to reduce exposure to a downtrending sector.
This plan aims for capital preservation, favoring income and broad index funds.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns. However, it also limits upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to 10% due to its downtrending status.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into an uptrending AI name.
Buy ANET at 8% to add a high risk/reward quality tech name.
This plan balances growth with risk management, reducing overall leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while still seeking growth from quality tech. Expected drawdowns are moderate, balancing risk and reward.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits.
Trim SOXL to 5% to maintain some leveraged exposure, despite its downtrend.
Sell SPAL completely as it is below its stop and a leveraged ETF.
Trim QQQI to 5% due to its downtrending status.
Increase CRWD to 20% to lean heavily into an uptrending AI name.
Buy NOW and ANET at 10% each, adding high-scoring scanner setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets around 4% per month, stretching for growth. It utilizes leveraged ETFs and concentrates in high-scoring setups, increasing both upside and potential drawdown. Be aware of daily decay in leveraged ETFs and the potential for rapid losses if stops are hit.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and make room for income.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income-focused exposure.
Sell CRWD to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Sell SMH to reduce high-beta sector exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income approach targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It maximizes exposure to income/covered-call ETFs and minimizes leverage and high-beta names. While prioritizing low drawdown, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
